reference_answer: Departing: Steven A. Lisi. Incoming: Robert Goodman. (Item 5.02, "Chief Executive Officer Transition".)
Delaware. (Cover-page jurisdiction table — left cell of the three-column row.)
001-38892. (Middle cell of the cover-page jurisdiction table.)

$650,000, representing Mr. Lisi's base salary as of date of separation, paid as compensation continuation over 12 months on the Company's regular payroll schedule, less withholdings. (Item 5.02, paragraph describing the Release Agreement terms.)
12 months. (Item 5.02, immediately following the $650,000 separation pay clause: "The Company shall also pay Mr. Lisi COBRA premiums for 12 months.")

Item 5.02 ("Departure of Directors or Certain Officers; Election of Directors; Appointment of Certain Officers; Compensatory Arrangements of Certain Officers"). Item 1.01 incorporates that description by reference rather than restating it.

Item 7.01 (Regulation FD Disclosure). The press release is furnished, not filed. The filing states the information under Item 7.01, including Exhibit 99.1, "shall not be deemed 'filed' for purposes of Section 18 of the Exchange Act, or incorporated by reference in any filing under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, or the Exchange Act, regardless of any general incorporation language in such filing, unless expressly incorporated by specific reference in such filing."

- Exhibit 10.1: Separation and Release of Claims Agreement by and between the Company and Steven A. Lisi dated March 27, 2026. - Exhibit 99.1: Press Release dated March 26, 2026. - Exhibit 104: Cover Page Interactive Data File (embedded within the inline XBRL document).

Item 5.02, in the paragraph announcing his CEO appointment: "Mr. Goodman was appointed as a director of the Company on June 16, 2025." The date appears once and is not repeated in the exhibit list or signature block.

The signature line reads "/s/ Daniel Moorehead" but the printed "Name:" line directly below reads "Daniel Moorhead" (without the second "e"). The two spellings of the CFO's surname disagree within the signature block. The document does not resolve which is correct.

All four boxes are unchecked (☐): - Written communication pursuant to Rule 425 under the Securities Act (17 CFR 230.425); - Soliciting material pursuant to Rule 14a-12 under the Exchange Act (17 CFR 240.14a-12); - Pre-commencement communications pursuant to Rule 14d-2(b) under the Exchange Act (17 CFR 240.14d-2(b)); - Pre-commencement communications pursuant to Rule 13e-4(c) under the Exchange Act (17 CFR 240.13e-4(c)).
Title: Common Stock, par value $.0001 per share. Trading Symbol: XAIR. Exchange: The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC. (Section 12(b) registration table on cover page.)

- WEP Clinical (Chief Commercial Officer, 2023 to 2024); - ActiGraph (2022 to 2023); - BioTelemetry, Inc. (acquired by Royal Philips) — including roles at BioTel Care and Alliance (Division President / Business Head) and BioTel Research (Senior Vice President of Global Sales and Marketing); more than nine years total; - Cardiocore (acquired by BioTelemetry) — senior leadership roles; - Thermo Fisher Scientific — senior leadership roles; - Pfizer — 15 years in progressively senior commercial positions. Current outside board: Fourth Frontier. Education: B.S. from Norwich University. Military: retired U.S. Army officer. Note: the BioTelemetry sentence contains a textual gap — "as Division President and Business Head of BioTel Care and Alliance from and Senior Vice President of Global Sales and Marketing at BioTel Research" — the word "from" appears to be missing a date or trailing phrase. Preserve verbatim.
See answer #8. Exhibit 10.1 is filed (under Item 1.01); Exhibit 99.1 is furnished (under Item 7.01); Exhibit 104 is the cover-page inline XBRL data file.

1. March 26, 2026 — Date of earliest event reported (cover page). 2. March 26, 2026 — Mr. Lisi notifies Board of his resignation. 3. March 27, 2026 — Mr. Lisi's resignation effective. 4. March 27, 2026 — Separation and Release of Claims Agreement executed. 5. Revocation Period: seven business days from March 27, 2026, excluding such date. 6. March 27, 2026 — All Mr. Lisi's unvested options and RSUs accelerate. 7. Twenty-four months from March 27, 2026 — Accelerated awards remain exercisable. 8. March 27, 2026 — Mr. Goodman's appointment as CEO effective. 9. June 16, 2025 — Mr. Goodman previously appointed as director. 10. November 2025 — Mr. Goodman has served as Chief Commercial Officer since this date. 11. March 26, 2026 — Company issued press release (Exhibit 99.1). 12. April 1, 2026 — Filing date / signature date.

The Revocation Period is seven business days from March 27, 2026, excluding such date. The Release Agreement "will only go effective after the Revocation Period ... has expired." Counting seven business days starting March 30 (the next business day after March 27, 2026, which was a Friday), the period would end approximately April 7, 2026, depending on holidays — the document does not give an explicit expiration calendar date. (Qualified answer: the duration is exact but the calendar end is derived, and "excluding such date" introduces a possible off-by-one.)

Acceleration is effective immediately as of March 27, 2026 (the date of separation). The accelerated awards "shall continue to remain exercisable for twenty-four (24) months from March 27, 2026," i.e., through approximately March 27, 2028.

Notification date: March 26, 2026. Effective date: March 27, 2026. (Item 5.02, paragraph 1.) There is therefore a one-calendar-day gap between notification and effectiveness — a detail an extractor must preserve, not collapse.
March 27, 2026 — the same date Mr. Lisi's resignation becomes effective. (Item 5.02.) The transitions are simultaneous, not sequential.

Two: (a) Mr. Lisi must have executed the Release Agreement (which "contains customary protections, including a general release of claims by Mr. Lisi in favor of the Company and certain other related parties"); and (b) the seven-business-day Revocation Period from March 27, 2026 must have expired without revocation. Only "after the Revocation Period" is the Company obligated to pay.

The Company has not yet entered into an employment agreement or made other compensation arrangements with Mr. Goodman. "As of the date of filing of this Current Report on Form 8-K, no material changes to Mr. Goodman's existing compensation arrangements have been made in connection with his appointment as Chief Executive Officer." The Company intends to begin negotiations and will disclose any agreement in a subsequent SEC report. This is a forward-looking commitment — the current 8-K does not commit Mr. Goodman to any specific compensation.

The filing affirmatively negates both: "There are no family relationships between Mr. Goodman and any director or executive officer of the Company, and there are no transactions between Mr. Goodman and the Company that require disclosure pursuant to Item 404 of Regulation S-K." These are negative-fact assertions, not absence-of-disclosure — the difference matters: the filing claims none exist, not that they were not investigated.

Item 1.01 contains only a pointer: "The description in Item 5.02 below, as it relates to the terms and conditions of the Separation and Release of Claims Agreement with Mr. Lisi, a copy of which is filed herewith as Exhibit 10.1, is incorporated herein by reference." The actual description is in Item 5.02, paragraphs 2 and 3. The full agreement is Exhibit 10.1. The 8-K therefore uses three coordinated locations for one agreement: Item 1.01 (the reference), Item 5.02 (the summary), and Exhibit 10.1 (the full text).

By a qualification clause: "The foregoing description of the Release Agreement is not complete and is subject to and qualified in its entirety by reference to the full text of the Release Agreement, which is attached as Exhibit 10.1 to this Current Report on Form 8-K and incorporated herein by reference." This is the standard SEC mechanism for handling a summary-vs-full-text mismatch: the summary in the body is non-binding to the extent it conflicts with the exhibit. An extractor that treats the Item 5.02 summary as authoritative may diverge from the actual agreement terms if Exhibit 10.1 says something different.

Cover page date of earliest event reported: March 26, 2026. The earliest event on that date is Mr. Lisi's resignation notification to the Board (which Item 5.02 places on March 26, 2026), and the Company's issuance of the press release (also March 26, 2026). The Separation Agreement was not executed until the next day (March 27, 2026), so its execution is a subsequent event covered by the same 8-K. The 8-K is triggered by the notification of resignation under Item 5.02(b) — that is the first reportable event in the chain. The press release (Item 7.01) happens contemporaneously; the Separation Agreement execution (Item 1.01 / Item 5.02) happens the following day. All three events fall within the four-business-day reporting window keyed to the March 26 notification date.
